title: pimpinella anthriscoides var. cruciata
intro: Pimpinella anthriscoides var. cruciata, also known as Pimpinella anisum var. cruciata, is a variety of aniseed or chervil, a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and carminative properties. The health benefits of Pimpinella anthriscoides var. cruciata include relieving digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, as well as reducing fever and aiding in the treatment of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Pimpinella anthriscoides var. cruciata can be made by drying the plant's leaves and seeds, then infusing them in hot water to create a tea or decoction for medicinal use.
